mainEntity.name: What is the time limit for filing income tax appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Appeal to CIT(A): 30 days from date of assessment order. Appeal to ITAT: 60 days from CIT(A) order. Condonation of delay is possible with valid reasons but not guaranteed — timely filing is critical.

mainEntity.name: Can I get a stay on income tax demand during appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. You can apply for stay of demand under Section 220(6) to the Assessing Officer, or file a stay petition with CIT(A)/ITAT. Generally, payment of 20% of disputed demand is expected for granting stay (CBDT Circular).

mainEntity.name: What are the fees for income tax appeal in Bangalore?

acceptedAnswer.text: Appeal fees depend on the complexity of issues, number of assessment years, and disputed amount. We offer a free initial review of your assessment order. Contact +91-95139 39333 for a fixed-fee quote after review.
